# Principles of Existential Therapy

**Principle 1: Confront the Ultimate Concerns. Death, freedom, isolation, meaning.**

**Principle 2: Be Authentic. The therapist must be human, not a blank screen.**

**Principle 3: The Relationship Heals. Therapy works through connection.**

**Case: Thelma's Illusion. She believes love will complete her. It will not.**

**Case: The Fat Lady's Shield. Weight protects her from intimacy.**

**Case: The Refusal. A patient refuses comfort. Yalom stays anyway.**

**Case: The Deathbed. Confronting death makes life more vivid.**
**Case: The Freedom. We are free to choose our response to any situation.**
**Case: The Isolation. Each of us is ultimately alone. Accepting this is liberating.**
**Case: The Meaning. There is no cosmic meaning. We must create our own.**
**Case: The Dream of Death. A patient dreams of a dark hole. It is her fear of nothingness.**
**Case: The Anxiety. Existential anxiety is not a symptom. It is a normal response to reality.**
**Case: The Meaning Crisis. Many patients come to therapy because their meaning system has collapsed.**
**Case: The Awakening. Confronting death can be an awakening that transforms how we live.**
**Case: The Courage. It takes courage to face the ultimate concerns. Therapy supports that courage.**
